title: "Documents you may need" interactive checklist (informational, no PII stored)
context: Benefits-Navigator is an openly-licensed corpus of plain-language, primary-source-cited, multilingual, accessible guides to public-assistance programs (SNAP, Medicaid/CHIP, WIC, TANF, LIHEAP, SSI, housing, EITC, Lifeline/ACP), delivered as a static site, printable PDFs, and structured data with an optional retrieval-grounded explainer. It is a HIGH risk-tier project: it provides information, not legal/benefits advice, never makes an individualized eligibility determination, and always routes people to the official application and free human help. No partner, credentialed reviewer, or requestor is yet secured. Future work: a "documents you may need" interactive checklist that is client-side only, never stores answers, and is explicitly not a determination - purely informational, helping people gather typical documents before they apply.
objective: Build a client-side "documents you may need" interactive checklist that stores no answers and makes no eligibility determination.
acceptanceCriteria: Checklist is client-side only and never stores or transmits answers (no PII, no tracking); privacy review confirms
Presents typically-needed documents informationally and explicitly states it is not an eligibility determination
Carries the fixed not-advice framing and routes to the official application and free human help
